code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.app_workloads.listWorkloads()
print(response)

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search and filter on the list based on the keys `name`, `title` (Display Name), `app_name` and `cluster_name`. All strings in the array are treated as _OR_. The search is case insensitive and will find partial matches as well.

parameters.name: app_names
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the key `app_name`. The filter is on the full name only.

parameters.name: app_versions
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the key `app_version`. The filter is on the full value only.

parameters.name: app_types
parameters.description: Filter Workloads by their application types.

parameters.name: cluster_names
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the key `cluster_name`. The filter is on the full name only.

parameters.name: node_names
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the key `node_name`. The filter is on the full name only.

parameters.name: workload_names
parameters.description: A filter on the list based on the key `name`. The filter is on the full name only.

parameters.name: resources
parameters.description: Filter based on Resource (KRN format) associated with the Workload. Supported KRNs: `asset`.

parameters.name: staged
parameters.description: A filter on the key staged. Using true will only show staged workloads, false will show all workloads.
